tape: Risk-off undercurrent persists and deepened slightly: Iran retaliated against US strikes by hitting Kuwait, Jordan and Qatar and the IRGC declared the Strait of Hormuz "closed until further notice," pushing Brent to ~$78.5-80, yet S&P 500 is only -0.3% and Nasdaq -0.7% (Dow +0.1%) with VIX actually down to 15.03 - equities still largely shrugging off the escalation ahead of Tuesday's CPI print.

alerts.headline: Iran-US conflict escalated further: Iran retaliated against Kuwait, Jordan and Qatar (widened from UAE/Qatar/Kuwait/Oman/Bahrain earlier), and IRGC says Strait of Hormuz is "closed until further notice." Brent ~$78.5-80. Equities resilient so far but tail risk is live into Tuesday's CPI.

Price data conflict: broker feed (14:41 UTC) shows AVO at $13.48 (flat, pnl +$0.02), but a web search returned a quote of $11.50 (-3.79%) - could be a stale/bad scrape rather than a real move. No corroborating news found for a drop. Bot should trust live broker price, not this search result.
+14.4% today continues; a Larimar director bought $167K in common stock on 7/8, adding to the insider-buying trend alongside the 6/29 BLA filing and 7/2 Wainwright Buy initiation. No negative news.

+4.9% today; still no fresh catalyst since the late-June EVO756 trial miss/downgrades (Morgan Stanley PT $26, Wainwright Neutral). New detail: company is pivoting EVO756 into a Phase IIb migraine-prevention trial, data expected 2027 - not an immediate catalyst.

No new negative news; backlog now $7.1B after ~$1.6B in fixtures since February (Petrobras, Equinor), and S&P upgraded credit to B- from CCC+ after ~$520M debt repayment. Susquehanna's $7 PT cut (already known) stands. Benefiting from Hormuz-driven oil spike.
CEO Ross Bhappu bought 74,000 shares (~$968K) on 7/7, additional insider buying on top of the 6/23 Vacuumschmelze acquisition. No fresh negative news.

Background context (not new today): Morgan Stanley/Wainwright PT raises to $10/$16 (6/25) and a $100M public equity offering announced 6/24 - the offering is a dilution event already priced in, not a fresh signal.

New top-scored candidate (6.5) is Viking Acquisition Corp II, a blank-check SPAC that closed its $230M IPO on 7/6 and just started trading 7/2 - no operating business yet, trades near $10 NAV. The high 'insider' score component likely reflects routine sponsor/founder share activity, not operating-company conviction.

No fresh news found tied to today; ticker is ambiguous in search (Caris Life Sciences quoted around $18.32 vs. broker's $16.95 for the held CAI position) - possible ticker collision, treat search results for CAI with caution.
Quiet; no news since 5/18 board/management insider buying. Earnings 7/29.
Quiet, no fresh news; earnings scheduled 8/12.

movers.note: +14.4% today, still riding BLA/coverage momentum plus a fresh 7/8 director buy.
+4.9% today despite unresolved late-June clinical downgrades - momentum without a new fundamental driver.
Lifted by the Hormuz-conflict oil price spike (Brent ~$78.5-80) plus its own credit upgrade and growing backlog.
